basic_details.job_title: BMC DNB Teacher Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Offline for 64 Posts
basic_details.post_name: Senior Consultant (DNB Teacher Grade-I) & Junior Consultant (DNB Teacher Grade-II) - Contract Basis
basic_details.short_information: Brihanmumbai Municipal Corporation (BMC) has released the official advertisement for filling in the posts of Senior Consultant (DNB Teacher Grade-I) and Junior Consultant (DNB Teacher Grade-II) on contract basis at its Peripheral Hospitals. A total of 64 vacancies have been announced across Shri Harilal Bhagwati Hospital, Borivali and Smt. Mansadevi Tulsiram Agarwal Hospital, Mulund under the DNB postgraduate programme. Eligible candidates aged up to 60 years with MD/MS/DNB qualification in the concerned speciality can apply offline from 21 April 2026 to 15 May 2026. The official notification

important_dates.application submission timings: Monday to Friday, 11:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m.

application_fee.details: Candidates are required to pay an application fee of ₹790/- plus GST via challan. The challan must be collected in person from the DNB Office at Rajawadi Hospital, Ghatkopar (East) during the stipulated dates and timings. The fee must then be paid at any CFC Centre of Municipal Ward Offices of BMC. | A photocopy of the fee payment receipt is mandatory and must be attached with the application form - applications submitted without the receipt will not be accepted under any circumstance.

age_limit.age_details: Up to 60 years (as on last date of application)
age_limit.details: The prescribed age limit for applying to BMC DNB Teacher Recruitment 2026 is up to 60 years, calculated as on the last date of application i.e. 15 May 2026. Age relaxation for reserved category candidates will be provided as per Government rules applicable to BMC / Maharashtra State Government norms. Candidates must attach valid proof of date of birth along with the application form.

qualification.education_qualification: MD / MS / DNB in the concerned speciality
qualification.details: Nationality: Citizen of India. | DNB Teacher Grade-I & Grade-II

salary_details.details: Selected candidates will be offered a fixed consolidated monthly remuneration based on the course type and grade of the post. The pay is structured to attract qualified and experienced medical professionals to contribute to DNB postgraduate medical education at BMC peripheral hospitals. No additional allowances or increments beyond the consolidated pay are mentioned in the official notification.

selection_process: 1. Application Scrutiny Applications checked for completeness, eligibility and required documents
2. Shortlisting Based on qualification, experience and prescribed eligibility criteria
3. Interview Date, time & venue communicated to eligible candidates via e-mail
4. Document Verification All original certificates to be produced at the time of interview
how_to_apply: The application process for BMC DNB Teacher Recruitment 2026 is completely offline. There is no online application portal for this recruitment. Candidates must download the official application form from the MCGM portal, fill it completely, collect and pay the challan fee, and submit the application in person or send it to the designated address before the closing date. Applications received after the closing date or without required documents will be summarily rejected without any intimation.
Download and print the application form and bio-data form from the MCGM portal at www.mcgm.gov.in.
Fill in all details completely and accurately in the prescribed format in BLOCK LETTERS.
Collect the challan of ₹790/- + GST from the DNB Office, Rajawadi Hospital, Ghatkopar (East) between 21.04.2026 and 15.05.2026 (Monday to Friday, 11:00 a.m. to 4:00 p.m.).
Pay the application fee at any CFC Centre of Municipal Ward Offices of BMC and retain the original payment receipt.

Attach self-attested photocopies of: Date of Birth proof, Domicile Certificate, School Leaving Certificate, all educational qualification certificates (UG & PG), experience certificates, caste certificate (if applicable), No Objection Certificate (if currently in Municipal Service), and Aadhaar Card copy.
Paste a recent passport-size photograph with self-attestation at the prescribed place on the application form.
Attach a photocopy of the fee payment receipt with the application - applications without receipt will not be accepted.

Submit the completed application in person at the Dispatch Section, Seth V. C. Gandhi & M. A. Vora Municipal General Hospital, Rajawadi Hospital, Ghatkopar (East) on or before 15 May 2026 between 11:00 a.m. and 4:00 p.m. (Monday to Friday).
Submit Offline Application to: The Chief Medical Superintendent & HOD (SHCS), Seth V. C. Gandhi & M. A. Vora Municipal General Hospital, Rajawadi Hospital, Ghatkopar (East), Mumbai.
important_instructions: Only self-attested copies of documents to be enclosed. Applications received after the closing date or without required documents will be rejected. The photocopy of the application fee receipt (challan) must be attached - applications without it will not be accepted. Candidates currently in Municipal Service must submit a No Objection Certificate from their Head of Department. Working knowledge of Marathi language is mandatory. Selected candidates may be transferred to any peripheral hospital under BMC jurisdiction where the DNB programme is proposed. The contract will be renewed every six months with a technical break. BMC reserves the right to amend, postpone or cancel the recruitment process without notice at any stage.

faq.question: Q1. What is BMC DNB Teacher Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: BMC DNB Teacher Recruitment 2026 is a contractual hiring drive by Brihanmumbai Municipal Corporation to engage Senior Consultant (DNB Teacher Grade‑I) and Junior Consultant (DNB Teacher Grade‑II) for DNB postgraduate teaching at its peripheral hospitals in Borivali and Mulund.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are released under BMC DNB Teacher Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: A total of 64 vacancies have been announced across Shri Harilal Bhagwati Hospital, Borivali and Smt. Mansadevi Tulsiram Agarwal Hospital, Mulund for DNB Teacher Grade‑I and Grade‑II positions in various degree and diploma specialities.

faq.question: Q3. Which hospitals are included in BMC DNB Teacher 2026 notification?
faq.answer: The vacancies are distributed between two BMC peripheral hospitals: Shri Harilal Bhagwati Hospital, Borivali and Smt. Mansadevi Tulsiram Agarwal Hospital, Mulund, where DNB postgraduate programmes are being run.

faq.question: Q4. What qualifications are required for BMC DNB Teacher Grade‑I and Grade‑II posts?
faq.answer: Candidates must possess MD, MS or DNB in the concerned speciality from a recognised institution, along with the prescribed post‑qualification teaching/clinical experience for Senior Consultant (Grade‑I) and Junior Consultant (Grade‑II) as per the course (degree or diploma) applied for.

faq.question: Q5. What is the age limit for BMC DNB Teacher Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The maximum age limit for both Senior Consultant and Junior Consultant posts is 60 years as on the last date of application, with age relaxations applicable for reserved category candidates as per Maharashtra Government and BMC rules.

faq.question: Q6. What is the salary for BMC DNB Teacher Grade‑I and Grade‑II?
faq.answer: BMC offers a fixed consolidated honorarium of around ₹2,00,000 per month for DNB Teacher Grade‑I (Senior Consultant) and ₹1,50,000 per month for DNB Teacher Grade‑II (Junior Consultant), depending on course type, without additional DA, HRA or other allowances.

faq.question: Q7. What is the application fee for BMC DNB Teacher Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Applicants must pay a non‑refundable application fee of ₹790 plus applicable GST through a challan collected from the designated DNB office and deposited at BMC’s CFC centres, and a copy of the payment receipt must be attached to the application form.

faq.question: Q8. What is the selection process for BMC DNB Teacher Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The selection process typically includes scrutiny of offline applications, shortlisting based on eligibility and experience, an interview of shortlisted candidates, and verification of original documents before issuing the final offer.
